gauge.name: Clear Fork (Big Coal) at Whitesville

impacts.statement: Several homes along Clear Fork Road flood, from the intersection with North Leevale Road to the mouth of Clear Fork. Areas of Whitesville near the river are flooded. Low areas along the Big Coal River are flooded down to Slyvester.
Home on Clear Fork Road across from North Leevale Road bridge floods.
Two homes on North Leevalle Road, just above the bridge, begin to flood.
North Leevale Road bridge and section of North Leevale Road just above the bridge flood.
Low areas in and around Whitesville flood.
Low spots on Clear Fork Road begin to flood. Two homes along the river on North Leevale Road, just above the bridge, flood between 23 and 25 feet.
From 20 to 23 feet, low areas near the river flood.

properties.caption: Bridge next to gage floods at 26 feet
Home at Clear Fork Rd. and South Leevale Rd. intersection floods at 30 feet
Clear Fork Rd. begins to flood at 26 feet and white colored home floods at 28 feet
Homes along North Leevale Rd., adjacent to creek, begin to flood at 24 feet
View of radar and wireweight gages

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are not available. Only observed stages are available for this point.
